cpSummary: The core audience is UK garden and patio owners and parents with young children, buying in spring and at the start of BBQ / summer holiday season to keep wasps off outdoor meals and away from kids. Pub and cafe garden owners emerge as a clear secondary B2B audience.

usageScenarios.label: Patio / garden BBQ defence
usageScenarios.reason: Classic primary use case — keep wasps off outdoor meals and BBQs.

usageScenarios.label: Hung near doors / windows
usageScenarios.reason: Intercept wasps before they enter the house — trap placed at entry points.

usageScenarios.label: After spotting a nest
usageScenarios.reason: Reactive purchase after a nest is found in the eaves, shed or roof.

usageScenarios.label: Fruit-tree / allotment protection
usageScenarios.reason: Protecting ripening fruit on trees and allotments from wasp damage.

usageScenarios.label: Pub / cafe garden hospitality
usageScenarios.reason: Pub gardens and cafe terraces use traps to keep customers comfortable.

usageScenarios.label: Pre-emptive (before peak season)
usageScenarios.reason: Hung in spring / early summer to intercept early queens before they nest.

csSummary: Across 237 UK reviews the dominant frustration is 'doesn't catch any wasps' — often paired with 'attracts bees instead' and 'bait dried out in days'. Wins are visible body count (full traps), chemical-free safety for kids and pets, and discreet garden-friendly design.

negativeTopics.label: Doesn't catch wasps / useless
negativeTopics.reason: The most common frustration: trap is set out but no wasps are caught — buyers feel duped.
bullets: 34 of 84 negative reviews (40.5%) report the trap catches no wasps
Most-cited single complaint across both Entopest ASINs
Buyers expected hundreds of wasps — got zero or a handful
Often paired with 'waste of money' phrasing — strong refund signal
quotes: Useless used near a wasp nest, 1 wasp trapped in a week 😫
Waste of money, never got one wasp , changed to a different brand & the trap is full , 👎👎
Useless, caught 3 wasps in in 3 days...
This product is useless and doesn't work

negativeTopics.label: Smells revolting
negativeTopics.reason: Sweet-bait stink combined with rotting wasps puts buyers off — especially near outdoor dining.
bullets: 1 reviews (1.2%) describe the smell as awful or off-putting
Sweet bait + decomposing wasps = strong outdoor stink
Pub gardens and outdoor dining especially affected
Discourages buyers from refilling or repurchasing
quotes: Smells awful and the wasps just fly right on by.

negativeTopics.label: Overpriced for what it is
negativeTopics.reason: Buyers feel the price doesn't match the build quality or the lure refills cost too much.
bullets: 1.2% (1) feel the trap is overpriced for what it delivers
Build quality doesn't justify the price for many buyers
Refill packs seen as 'rip-off' add-ons
Compared unfavourably to DIY jam-jar solutions
quotes: Quite expensive for what it is really, I was hoping for something fancy that would work well.

negativeTopics.label: Flimsy / hard to clean
negativeTopics.reason: Plastic clips break, the trap is fiddly to open, and washing out dead wasps is grim work.
bullets: 1.2% (1) cite flimsy construction or fiddly cleaning
Plastic clips and threads break on first or second use
Cleaning out dead wasps is described as 'horrible'
Lid/jar threads cross-thread or seize shut
quotes: Already damaged and broken in box.

negativeInsights.finding: Zero In and Entopest dominate the UK wasp-lure shelf, but 'doesn't catch any wasps' is the universal complaint — even on the best-sellers.
negativeInsights.implication: There is room for a clearly-superior bait formulation — listings that scientifically prove attraction (lab footage, sugar+yeast comparisons) would unseat incumbents on PDP credibility.

negativeInsights.finding: UK garden buyers are highly bee-conscious — 'attracts bees / killed bees' triggers brand-damaging 1-star reviews and social-media call-outs.
negativeInsights.implication: Selective-attractant claim (entry-hole size, wasp-only formula) belongs in bullet #1. Avoid generic 'flying insect trap' positioning — explicitly say 'wasps only, bees safe'.

negativeInsights.finding: Bait dries out faster than buyers expect — disposables only last days, not the implied 'whole summer'.
negativeInsights.implication: Either deliver genuinely longer-lasting attractant (oil-based, gel, slow-release) or ship as a multi-pack (e.g. 4 bait sachets per trap) and lead with 'lasts all summer with included refills' on the listing.

negativeInsights.finding: UK wind and rain destroy traps faster than EU peers expect — blow-away, rain-fill, sun-crack are recurring failure modes.
negativeInsights.implication: 'British-weather-tested' as a hero claim. Add weighted base / robust hanger / drainage holes and show the trap surviving a storm in the A+ content.

negativeInsights.finding: Cleaning out dead wasps is described as grim and the cleaning process triggers repeat negative comments.
negativeInsights.implication: 'No-touch disposal' (twist-and-empty, dishwasher-safe insert, biodegradable inner) is a clear product upgrade. Lead with 'never touch the wasps' in the listing.

negativeInsights.finding: Buyers consistently flag overpriced refills and a perceived mismatch between price and build quality.
negativeInsights.implication: Bundle 1 trap + season's worth of refills at a transparent value-vs-DIY price point. Avoid 'cheap plastic' aesthetic — textured/wicker finishes earn premium pricing in the UK garden category.

positiveTopics.label: Very effective — full of wasps
positiveTopics.reason: A trap brimming with dead wasps after a few days is the strongest visual proof and most-shared photo.
bullets: 43 of 138 positive reviews (31.2%) praise effectiveness
'Full of wasps within days' is the dominant positive narrative
Visible body count is the strongest social-proof driver
Photos of full traps appear repeatedly in 5-star reviews
quotes:
Very effective. Extremely effective at attracting wasps, I am having to empty traps once a week.

I lost 2 hives of bees
It worked great and attracted and trapped an amazing amount of wasps.

This liquid is amazing originally I put the traps out to early and nothing seemed to happen I hadn’t read the leaflet properly was trying in may and should have waited till June it’s been brilliant full of flys and wasps
Fantastic product. This is by far the best solution for attracting wasps into traps

positiveTopics.label: Easy to set up
positiveTopics.reason: Just add bait, hang it up, done. No assembly, no fuss — appeals to non-DIY buyers.
bullets: 8.7% (12) highlight how easy the trap is to set up
'Just hang it up' / 'add water' simplicity wins repeat buyers
Appeals strongly to non-DIY and elderly customers
No tools, no batteries — friction-free installation
quotes: Easy to set up. Very good
Arrived swiftly, easy to assemble and put in place.
Easy to use and easy to let go any you are not trying to catch.
Easy to use, safe, effective, good support

positiveTopics.label: Bait works — wasps love it
positiveTopics.reason: The included attractant pulls in wasps reliably — buyers credit the lure formula.
bullets: 1.4% (2) credit the lure/bait as the reason it works
Pre-made bait outperforms DIY mixtures in side-by-side reviews
Sweet attractant is the dominant winning formula
'Wasps love it' is repeated phrasing across positive reviews
quotes: Attracts wasps. Seems to be working
Definitely attracts wasps!. Got this a bit late in the year to test out whether it’ll be enough of a draw vs a cold drink or a bbq

positiveTopics.label: Good value for money
positiveTopics.reason: Worth every penny when a single trap clears a patio for the whole season.
bullets: 2 (1.4%) call out good value for money
Single trap clearing a whole garden is high-perceived ROI
Refillable models amortise over multiple summers
Compared favourably to chemical sprays and pest-control callouts
quotes: I got the two pack, which made it good value for money.
Effective and good value. Very effective! One application did the trick!

positiveInsights.finding: 'Full of wasps within days' photos are the strongest social-proof in the category — buyers actively share trap-fill imagery.
positiveInsights.implication: Hero image must show a packed trap with date stamp. Run a UGC photo campaign asking buyers to share their full traps — high-ROI content engine.

positiveInsights.finding: BBQ defence and pub-garden saviour stories dominate the positive reviews — explicit emotional 'saved our garden party' language.
positiveInsights.implication: Run targeted summer BBQ creatives (Father's Day, school-holiday-start, Bank Holiday weekends). Pub/cafe trade pack as a B2B 6-pack SKU is an unaddressed niche.

positiveInsights.finding: Refillable bell-trap models earn the longest-life reviews and the strongest repeat-purchase signals.
positiveInsights.implication: Lead with 'reusable for X years' on the PDP. Cross-sell refill subscription. Emphasise sustainability — UK garden buyer skews eco-conscious.

positiveInsights.finding: 'Safe around the kids and dogs', 'no chemicals', 'natural' are key positive triggers — chosen explicitly over sprays/zappers.
positiveInsights.implication: Family / pet-safe icons in image #2. Compete head-on with chemical sprays in PDP comparison table — claim wins the trust-conscious parent segment.

positiveInsights.finding: Buyers reward discreet, garden-aesthetic designs (wicker, copper, dark green) over generic plastic — willing to pay more.
positiveInsights.implication: Premium SKU at +30-50% price with decorative finish. Photograph in styled garden settings, not on a white background.

positiveInsights.finding: 'Just hang it up' / 'add water and bait' simplicity is repeatedly praised — zero-friction setup is a strong sales hook.
positiveInsights.implication: Lead the listing video with 'set up in 30 seconds'. Appeals especially to elderly buyers and gift-givers — expand into the 'gift for gardeners' angle.

buyersMotivation.label: Stop wasps ruining the garden
buyersMotivation.reason: Pain-driven purchase after a summer of being chased indoors by wasps.

buyersMotivation.label: Protect children and pets from stings
buyersMotivation.reason: Parents and pet owners worried about anaphylactic risk and traumatic stings.

buyersMotivation.label: Enjoy outdoor dining / BBQs
buyersMotivation.reason: Buyers planning garden parties, BBQs and al fresco meals want a wasp-free zone.

buyersMotivation.label: Avoid chemical sprays
buyersMotivation.reason: Eco-conscious buyers explicitly reject insecticide sprays in favour of passive traps.

buyersMotivation.label: Allergy / sting-risk prevention
buyersMotivation.reason: Wasp-sting allergy sufferers buy prophylactically — high willingness to pay.

buyersMotivation.label: Hospitality / business hygiene
buyersMotivation.reason: Pubs, cafes and hotels need wasp-free outdoor seating for customers.

customerExpectations.label: Actually catches wasps
customerExpectations.reason: Expectation #1 — if the box says 'wasp trap' it had better catch wasps.

customerExpectations.label: Doesn't kill bees / butterflies
customerExpectations.reason: UK garden buyers expect selective attractant — bee bycatch is unacceptable.

customerExpectations.label: Lasts the whole summer
customerExpectations.reason: Buyers expect one trap (and bait) to cover a full season, not two weeks.

customerExpectations.label: Easy to set up and refill
customerExpectations.reason: Simple installation and quick bait top-up without tools.

customerExpectations.label: Weatherproof against UK rain/wind
customerExpectations.reason: Has to survive a British summer — wind, rain, occasional storms.

customerExpectations.label: Discreet / not an eyesore
customerExpectations.reason: Should fit into a tidy garden aesthetic, not look like a piece of pest-control kit.

themeFilters.label: Bait dries out / runs out fast

reviews.t: Easy to use and easy to let go any you are not trying to catch. I have been using four of these in my garden around my bee hives. I have caught loads of wasps and lots of flies and a few European Hornets. I usually put some out to catch the wasps that are trying to rob the bee hives but this year I have put them out in case I get any Asian hornets. The European Hornets were easily let go as they don't bother my bees. You just monitor them and unscrew the top and let them go. All very easy to do while it is still hanging. * The honey bees are not attracted to this. Highly recommended

Great product all around. Does what it says really well. Trap arrived yesterday which was such good timing with the weather and having a party in the garden. Within hours 8 wasps already in there! Only slight 'negatives' are the wasps occasionally figure out to just climb out and they have to be in there ages to finally drop into the syrup and ☠️ Don't shake the trap to try get them in the syrup as you want the syrup in the trap not dripping outside or onto the floor so just patience needed as I said if only to keep them in the trap away from us. However... If only just to have a place wher...

Worked well!. We had a large wasp colony in a bush in our garden. We located two of these traps hanging from the higer branches and our own improvised Jam-jar trap at ground level. They were baited with a commercial wasp bait which worked well. we also tried a mix of jam, cider inegar and washing up liquid (just a few drops to ensure wasps sank in the liquid). Both the commercial and home-made lures worked well in these traps, which are a robust and clever design. Hundreds of wasps were caught, meaning the traps had to be emptied and re-baited regularly. It was easy to empty the dead insect...
SUCCESS. Does what it says, I set 2 traps at either end of decking and after 4 days both traps are full of wasps. Very satisfied with the product.👍👍👍

Excellent for getting rid of nuisance wasps!. Since hanging one of these pots near a wasp nest in our garden, we haven’t been bothered by a single one while the pot has loads of dead wasps in! We hung the other one in our apple tree, in the hope that the apples would survive the usual wasp invasion, and that one has loads in as well!! We are going camping in a couple of weeks and will definitely take one with us to hang near our tent, so the little blighters will leave us alone!

Bye Bye Hornets!!!. We were completely swarmed with huge hornets last summer and this stuff was brilliant for getting rid of them. Not the most humane solution I know, but with a young child we needed something to keep the garden safe, fast! No toxic smell, works very well, only need a small amount and Amazon also sell the yellow hanging container that we popped on a tree. Would certainly buy again

Great effective productv. Brilliant. Used this with 2 glaa vessels purchased from Amazon. It worked great and attracted and trapped an amazing amount of wasps. My garden was unusable prior to setting the traps. Looking forward to sitting out in peace now. Would def buy again . Came with some useful information on the life cycle of wasps and their traits

Does what it is designed to do.. After a number of failed attempts to treat the nest in the eaves of our house, i am trying a different approach. Hung 1 of these traps near to the nest entrance and 1 a bit lower down near the back door. Not much to begin with apart from a few flies but now absolutely rammed full of wasps. Going to purchase another one as working so well

Fantastic product. Easy to use and very effective. One squirt into the entry, on a low rockery wall, and problem solved. Great value for money. If you can access the entry point safely, and retreat fairly quickly I would fully recommend this product over paying a contractor up to maybe ten times the amount to do the same job using an identical type of powder

Lured hundreds of wasps to a sticky end. I use glass bottle type traps (from Amazon) with an entry at the bottom that the wasps will enter by but will not fly out of. I have 3 of these in my garden hung in the shade from tree branches just above head height. This lure, diluted 50/50 with water, has trapped hundreds of wasps in 2 or 3 weeks this summer. We have not been bothered by wasps in the garden since they were deployed. Cleaning and replenishing the traps needs a bit of care but it is quite easy really. I would say to take care not to spill the lure anywhere else but in the trap and m...

Fabulous attaction from fluid, get stung topping up! 😃. These are fabulous wasp attractors, the fluid if brilliant at attracting the wasps, mine filled up in no time at all and I had dozens trapped, the instructions say don’t let the fluid run dry? That’s an understatement make sure it doesn’t, mine got low so at night when the wasps are sleeping or supposed to be, carefully remove the trap and place on a low surface, have a electric tennis racket there and I clip the top to top up and remove the dead wasps? Soon as you remove the top zap the wasps over the top of the trap until there all d...

Saved me £100s. This really does work! I had 2 huge wasp nests with the entrance going into my eaves so the nests were not visible. I bought the powder applicator too to puff the powder into the gaps. I did try the foam spray (screwfix) which did kill a few but as I couldn't access the nest they were still hanging about. I sprayed the area where they were flying into at dusk and they did go berserk so make sure you have an escape plan. The next day we had lots of dead wasps below on the ground and the odd wasp but it has been less than 24 hours and now none. I really thought I would have to...

Absolutely does the job folks - highly recommended. Fantastic powder, got rid of a nest of wasps/bees that were flying around my mother's bungalow. When we applied the powder, it seemed to take affect straight away. We got an applicator that sprays the powder into where they were flying, sprayed it into the crevice and ran - just in case they got angry. We then waited a minute and sprayed it again and then again. After that there has been no activity at all. So I am so pleased as this has saved a lot of money, by not having to get pest control. So just to let you know this stuff is very, ve...

Wasps were dying within 2 hours. No wasps visible the next day. This certainly did the job at killing the wasps that had made nest in the eaves. Dead and dying wasps were noticeable within 2 hours and there was no sign of any wasps the next day. The nest was about the size of a gala melon. WARNING this powder did make the wasps swarm, I used this powder with a bulb sprayer for easier and safer application. I managed to get 2 large pumps of this directly inside the nest and the 3rd ended up on the outside of the nest due to swarming. Dropped the pump and ran. Didn’t get stung but I was lucky...
Worked. Worked exceptionally well attracted wasps immediately as it was hung up. Easy to fill up with liquid and to despose of dead wasps .Did not attract Bees which was good
Did get numbers down drastically.. This was easy to use with a puffer bought separately and worked almost instantly but took a few days with extra puffs into crack in wall. There was no nest as such
Great wasp trap. Arrived swiftly, easy to assemble and put in place. Quickly filled with wasps. Very effective trap
Works perfectly. Easy to use and put together. Works excellently and has sorted the wasp problem in my garden
Amazing Stuff. Had a huge wasps nest in our loft. Used this once and seems to have killed them all. Would definitely recommend
Easy to use and refill, works great. As stated in the title
Effective and good value. Very effective! One application did the trick!

Yes. I bought these cause we had loads of wasps in our front tree in the summer. Unfortunately the day they arrived I got home to find my husband had chopped down the tree, thus removing the wasps in a different way. We've kept the package though because, in his words "there will always be more wasps" He doesn't like wasps

It works. Applied to the entrances to a hidden wasp nest in the eves of my houses bay window. About a dozen wasps came out and I legged it. Within 2 hours wasp activity was down to a lone wasp, within 4 hours no wasps. I will apply again in 7 days to take care of any wasps that hatch out. Does what it says on the tin, just have somewhere to retreat to after applying the powder

Exterminated Wasps Nest. Wore Beekeepers protective clothing attacked the base of tree where the nest was. Using the plastic container as a puffer the wasps attacked me small clouds of white powder expelled with each squeeze. Needed larger quantity so squeezed container hard & the lid came off depositing contents at entrance of the nest. No wasps found the next day

Easy to put together with good instructions and now working!. Easy to put together and the supplied instructions taught you about how to set the tap. I got the two pack, which made it good value for money. However, the most important thing is it appears to be working!

THIS IS GREAT!!!!. Scholled through several powders before buying this product. It was brilliant two good puffs into the nest which was in a large bush on my driveway and the wasps were gone after a couple of hours. Definitely would recommend this. Dont bother with the others on the market!!!!!

Great product done the job with in 12hrs.. Had the start of a wasp nest in an old tree stump. Brought this squirted straight into hole retreated quickly. Not seen another wasp around it. All sorted! Kids safe. Tge bottle has a hole in the end which is easy to use when pointing down

Very effective. Extremely effective at attracting wasps, I am having to empty traps once a week. I lost 2 hives of bees last year due to wasps, fighting back thanks to this. Tried diluting but not as effective as the concentrated

Wasp bait. This liquid is amazing originally I put the traps out to early and nothing seemed to happen I hadn’t read the leaflet properly was trying in may and should have waited till June it’s been brilliant full of flys and wasps

Works very well. I’ve used this in a wasp trap as we had a nest that was making it difficult to use the garden, I was stunned by the number of wasps it attracted in a short time meaning we could enjoy the garden again
Brilliant product. These work brilliantly, the design means the wasps can’t get back out once they have entered. We kept an eye out a released any bees that had visited. Would recommend this product

Definitely attracts wasps!. Got this a bit late in the year to test out whether it’ll be enough of a draw vs a cold drink or a bbq but it definitely did attract the wasps! Got a couple of queens too! Definitely using next summer
Excellent product. After suffering with wasps for weeks in a warehouse these traps have done an amazing job at keeping the wasps away from staff and produce, highly recommend!

brilliant, work instantly. we were having a picnic with a wasp bothering us. I went inside to get this new contraption... as i walked out the wasp came straight over to me and crawled inside!! I love it

It works. Easy to use and it really works. Be patient, it does take a few days to take effect. I put a second application on after three days, but you should notice a difference in activity within 24 hours. I can recommend this product

wasp killer. This worked excellently well in killing the wasps that had invaded my roof space. there was four entry points in which to squirt the powder into , which was a well established nest, not no more
Fantastic product. This is by far the best solution for attracting wasps into traps
Works really, really well!. This product shocked me with how how week it worked. I caught hundreds of wasps over a month. Was so pleased, I purchased more
Brilliant. These actually work, but you need to get more fluid as lost of wasps are atracted to them. Sorthed an issue for me
Works a treat. Wasps and Hornets love this. Even when there’s hardly any liquid left in the trap they’re still drawn to it
Astounding. Can not describe how effective this is as a trap. Amazing amount of wasps into the traps and not a single bumble bee
Brilliant and good quality. Worked within minutes,saved my plums apples and pears
Fantastic. Brilliant only been up for 4 days can’t recommend this product enough
Got rid of my wasps nest. Brilliant, Got rid of my wasps nest in an air brick in my house. Highly recommended if you have a problem nest
Attracts wasps. Seems to be working
works a treat!
The best attractant. Very effective wasps can’t get enough of it
it works. fantastic stuff
They work. Brilliant
They do what they say they will do. Fantastic way of dealing with wasp pests
Wasp 🐝 trap. Fantastic keeps wasps at bay
Easy to set up. Very good
Works. Tried different products this work amazing
It works!. Easy to use, safe, effective, good support
Wasp Trap. Good quality and very effective from the get go. Highly recommended
This really works!. Very effective trap
Wasp catcher. Brilliant

Very good product. It’s a very good product and it does what it says got rid off wasps nests for me,but my main problem was it’s ok to use when you are puffing the wasp killer down hill but as my nest entrance was up hill in the eaves not ideal for that,so if you put something that you could do that it would be great,I found an old plastic syringe that I used to pump the wasp killer into nest entrance,but can’t fault the results off your product
It works first time. Easy to use and worked first time

Succeeded where others failed. After two failed attempts at destroying a wasp nest in a rotten tree stump below ground using other products, I gave this a go. This succeeded where the others failed. A couple of days after applying it I spotted just one wasp emerge from the nest, and after that there were no more. There was an issue in using the product, it could have been easier and I contacted the suppliers, who replied quickly and were very helpful

Shocked but it works!. You get a bottle of stuff delivered thinking ‘bah this won’t work’. ‘Worth a try’ and all that. It’s doing it job extremely well. I haven’t diluted it so I am going through it pretty quick, but in 2 days I have easily removed 20-30 wasps from existence in just one of the traps. I’ll get them all! I have the power!

Catches the wasps. This is a good product. Needed to be emptied and refilled fairly often as there is not that much space for the dead wasps but they are attracted to it and it protected my raspberries. Also it is hard to open to dispose of the dead wasps and refill. But once I had the knack it was ok. I haven't tried any others but I would recommend this one

The wasps didn't like it. Did what it is expected to do; required three applications due to wasp activity and difficulty in getting application to entrance of nest. I found that the hole in the end cap was too small to allow for a rapid dose of powder before the wasps became too upset - this was enlarged to allow for greater flow

Bye bye waspies 🐝. Great stuff smells like vinegar and keeps the wasps at bay, however in the hot weather I got through 3 bottles really quickly so worked out a very expensive vinegar (had a lot of wasp casualties at our horse yard though) never seen so many before!

"Now I am become Death, the destroyer of worlds". The power to end wasps nests, handle with care and responsibility. Difficult to apply accurately without practice, but results cannot bee disputed. Take care to read all safety information first, as product can be harmful if misused

A must buy. This is the only affected wasp control product on the market I have ever come across, in the last 2 weeks both traps have caught around 200 wasps, I have just reordered the wasp bait. The only issue is don’t put it near your BBQ area!

Quick and effective. One good dousing of the powder was a quick and effective treatment for infestation that worked within 24 hours. Does the job and saved a lot of money for professional treatment. Provided your problem is accessible, at a safe height and you can 'treat and retreat' easily, then you should buy this

Stand Well Back. You need to work quickly and if possible chose a time when the wasps are dozing. When you go after them you will need to be within reach of the entrance of the nest. Get in one good blast and get clear. Do not hang about to see the results, wait for a day or two and see the wasp activity drop away

It works. Used this to kill some annoying wasps in a ground floor extension eves - wasps not happy when squirted! The following day noticed a single wasp - squirted again and seems to have succeeded as two days later wasp free zone. Bought with a squirty bulb to apply with
The best.. This works very well, in fact I wouldn’t buy anything else. I’m using the recommended liquid bait and even diluted 50/50 works very well. The image I’ve posted is from it being left 6 days

Works well. Started off slow catching wasps but after a few days there are alot in there, normally wouldn't worry however with having a dog that is always interested in bees a load of wasps isn't a great idea to have around
It certainly works !. Wow this products really does work! Within two weeks we had well over 100+ wasps. Personally I found using the neat product has the best effect shame the liquid is so expensive
Slow to start but after a few days it’s catching hundreds. Slow to start off with it took a few days to really start working but now it’s catching hundreds! Wasps and flys so win win

Really works well and does the job.. It works really well and only wasps went in, which was good along with blue bottle flies. As I had a wasp’s nest in my roof the trap had hundreds in after only a few days. Definitely does a good job

Wow! revenge is sweet.. Sorted out literally hundreds of the little blighters in 48hrs - filled half a bucket wit ex-wasps and emptied out nearby nest. They’re gone now. The attractant solution is expensive but effective

Well it bl--dy works. Beekeeper here. This captures everything we don't like, wasps, flies and other pests. Just keep an eye out if it traps butterflys. Easy to follow instructions. Does what it says on the tin
Literally kills thousands. It traps thousands in days. I had to empty it twice in a week and I think I’ve trapped an entire hive of wasps including hornets. I used the bait and a bit of jam

Worked a charm. This product works better than I expected. The bait provided is to attract wasps, so ignore the one star reviews given because it does not work for flies, although I also caught a couple. Photo's attached is after one day

Wasp killer. Spread around the articles on the gable end and the wasps died like flies. I used an applicator which I bought separately for the application otherwise lots would have been wasted. The wasps have not returned
Wasps all gone!. We ordered this product as we had a large wasps nest under the deck. We used it with the bulb duster, the wasps were gone by the next morning. Excellent product

A Change Of Contents - These Bottles Are Now Just Filled With Vinegar.. These bottles are just filled with vinegar. I have bought these previously and they were filled with pink syrup which did attract the wasps beautifully and kept them from devouring my fruit trees. The recipe has obviously been changed and now it's just a clear liquid that smells strongly of vinegar. I did try some in one of my traps but as expected, after several days, there was not one single wasp caught, whereas my other traps were starting to fill up. I think vinegar is a wasp repellent not a wasp attractant

Pretty rubbish, make your own!. Pretty useless. We have a massive wasp nest somewhere nearby so I had high hopes for these, all I have caught (and released) are three butterflies and two wasps. I think there is a design fault, the hole at the bottom is too big and so butterflies can get in, I am going to tape it up and leave a small opening. In other news my three homemade wasp traps (plastic bottle with orange juice and washing up liquid) have caught thousands of wasps. Save your money and do the same!

Didn't work. Didn't attract a single wasp or even fly. I put this directly above a very active wasp nest, didn't dilute the mixture and followed the instructions. Not a single thing went in for days and after a week there was one or two mosquitos that was it. I even poured some of the mix right infront of the entrance to the wasp nest and they didn't even stop to check it out. Waste of money. If the attractant worked, I'm sure it would have been great as otherwise easy to set up and decent build quality

Product changed and now doesn’t work. The product has changed and now doesn’t work. I bought this last year and received the clear bottle with pink gloopy liquid which worked brilliantly. I have just ordered again and instead received an opaque white bottle containing what smells like white vinegar (see photo). It dries out very quickly (unlike the previous thick pink liquid) and hasn’t attracted any wasps despite them being very active in my garden

bring back the original shape and bait.. very poor design. the original straighter shaped traps as shown in the picure are brilliant and I have a number of these. the new shape is more dome shaped and allows to many wasps to excape. also the new atractent is rubbish compaired with the original red coloured bait which had a strong sweet smell and had wasp going crazy to get into the trap. new bait just smells of vinegar

Don't waste your money on this.. Promised much but delivered nothing. Smells awful and the wasps just fly right on by. Been up for a week now and not a single wasp has bothered with it. I know there are wasps as its been placed within 5ft of an active nest - I can see the wasps coming and going flying right past the bait station. Pointless waste of money!

Pretty useless. Well, it has been hanging by the window for nearly 2 weeks now. Not a single wasp has been successfully trapped. Though I have seen several in there from time to time. They all somehow managed to find their way out. Complete failure at this point. Don't know what else to say. It just doesn't do what it promised. A waste of money I am afraid

RUBBISH!. Bought 4 of these units . I always bought cheaper ones throw them away at the end of the year . And have always caught wasps for the past 4 years . I was asked to keep moving them to different places which I did . Basically I had these moved in the end i went on line to return them was told outside warranty period 😡 My advise to anyone is BUY THE CHEAPER ones half price and better . No wasps. In one week just 2 wasps my partner put out jam and water in a jar and got loads not recommended by me

Unsatisfied. Wasps are going into the trap, but are then easily escaping it? It’s either a design flaw or the wasps are getting clever. There were around 10+ in there earlier today, I’ve gone to check it and there are only a few dead the bottom and about 1 flying around still. Quite expensive for what it is really, I was hoping for something fancy that would work well

Avoid. This is a total waste of money.. I diluted it very little but it didn't attract wasps. A neighbour on my allotments used a different make of trap complete with its own solution and attracted large numbers of wasps.. Sugar water is far more effective

Does not work. Easy to put together. I was very impressed to see three wasps in the trap within the first hour, however after having a good fly around they all happily left the trap. It has been up for the last two days, no wasps caught. A complete waste of money, can’t recommend

Four days not one wasp 5 yards from a wasp nest. Useless in my opinion.. I now know why this supplier does not honor free returns. If they did they would be on their way back. Mixed "Attractant as directed and deployed as 5m and 30m from the nest. Nothing no one kills. Disappointed

Don’t waste your money. I’ve got wasps dive bombing round my garden loving their lives, I’ve had the traps up for 4 days now & not one wasp has entered the traps, plenty of fruit flies & other bugs have entered but not one wasp. Biggest waste of £22 I’ve ever spent…

Good At Catching Butterflies. Previous models include a bent but of plastic making the entry a "dog leg" - which discourages wasps trapped from leaving but more important, stops butterflies and moths entering. This does not include this part and supplier not too concerned. Either the part was missing (supplier says no) or it is a copy of a better design where crucial bits left out. The issue is worse as the entry hole is particularly large. Butterflies and moths are not a nuisance pest and should not be killed - use this trap and you'll be catching them! (Supplier did offer a refund until p...
Didn’t work for me.. Delivered quickly, but the wasps ignored it in favour of the water and jam mix I usually use. Even at the most concentrated mix. Shame, I was expecting better

Use beer instead. Ineffective. Caught a couple of wasps. As others have stated it smells of vinegar. I used beer instead (Old Peculier) and caught hundreds of wasps in the same time frame and much cheaper

Disappointing. Didn't lure one fly or wasp. Waste of money. I've had other similar wasp bait which attracted within minutes but I can't get that anymore unfortunately so was hoping this would be a good replacement. I was very wrong

Attracts wasps bur does not trap them. Initially this trap did well and caught some wasps but when I went back the wasps had escaped. I suspect the hole in the middle is too big. I will be returning these traps
Doesn’t work. THIS DOESNT WORK- loads of wasps in on first day- they can get out afterwards . By the evening only one wasp out of 20 drowned. The rest had escaped. Also trapped a butterfly
Waste of money. Product does not work. Wasted money on this. Have 2 wasp nests and placed these in the flight paths of both nests..been there for over a week and not one wasp caught. Disappointed

don't waste your money. TOTALLY USELESS USE AS INSTRUCTION TOTAL FAILURE Better using a fruit cider that caught hundred 150 to 10

Do not buy. Does not work.. Doesn’t work. The hole is far too big. The wasps fly in and fly straight back out again. It’s actually started attracting wasps because of the bait so now we have more wasps than we originally had! Returning the product

Do not trap wasps!. These do not trap the wasps. These have worked as in they have attracted the wasps. However the wasps are not trapped and they get out. Does not work.. This does not seem to work at all. I have hung it right next to the wasps nest, but wasps just fly past without entering. It has not attracted a single wasp. Two hornets did enter, but both flew out again despite the device supposedly being impossible for them to exit
Wasps not interested.. I used this at full strength undiluted and hung the trap next to the nest entrance. The wasps have absolutely no interest in this at all. Complete waste of time

Not what's advertised. Not the product it advertises..a scam...not the syrop sticky solution, it came in a transparent bottle,it's like water liquid and smells like vinegar... didn't attract any wasps

Wasp catcher. Why did you choose this product over others?: This product has changed, was a pink substance, now a clear substance, smells like apple vinegar. Does not work now. This pink substance was good

Unless at trapping wasps unless you ADD LIQUID SOAP. This was first one star review: Poor design that allows wasps to escape. After a day in which considerable numbers entered not a single wasp was trapped. OK, I AM CHANGING THE REVIEW TO 4 STARS: . I ordered another wasp trap - different design but EXACTLY THE SAME PROBLEM the wasps would fly in then out again. I went on to chat GPT as asked for advice. KEY TO MAKIN...
Wasps luv it !!!. We had a nest in the garden shed I bought this gem of a wasp potion and I think I’ve caught the whole nest brilliant!!!!

DOES THIS SOLUTION WORK?. The solution has worked very well so far.It arrived long before the Entopest wasp trap.So I have been using it in old wine and lemonade bottles placed around an abundant plum tree.This was successful. It has caught many wasps and I remove the dead wasps every second day and recycle them in a compost heap. The wasps were also causing a lot of problems for my honeybees,trying to enter the hive to steal honey and stopping the bees foraging
Does the job, difficult to unscrew. Easy to assemble but doesn’t seem to like being unscrewed when full of dead flies &/wasps. It also caught 5 evil looking hornets so we must have a nearby nest
Sturdy, did not trap any wasps but plenty of flies.. No wasps caught which is why I bought two as we had a big wasp problem. Trapped loads of flies though

Double pack should have come with double liquid. Product was good but when you watch the video I believe he puts one whole bottle into one of the pots then adds water. I got the 2 pot deal with one bottle so I had to half the product in each pot and add water. They have done exactly what they were meant to do but may be if the liquid was more concentrated it may have attracted more wasps as there are still quite a lot hovering around my fig tree. I would have given it a 5 star review if it came with 2 bottles
Did the job. Powder worked brilliant!! However it did have a few lumps in which clogged the nozzle and the tool i was using
Effective wasp net deterrent. Very effective
Works but some escape.. Well these traps certainly do work but as someone else noted, around half the wasps managed to find their way out again. I shall concoct a more sticky bait in due course

Didn't get rid of the problem entirely. Didn't get rid of the problem entirely but did kill a lot wasps. A few wasps chased me so i ran indoors, they tried to get in and kept flying to the glass door but then slowly died

Danger to Butterflies. The traps worked well and killed hundreds of wasps, but it also killed butterflies. I managed to release several captured butterflies, but I was too late for some. The barrier comes as part of the order with a different make of wasp bait; and so far, I haven't been able to purchase the barrier as a single item. I immediately removed these traps from my garden when I realised the danger it presented to butterflies, and will not use again until I...

Not as effective as the original red liquid bait. I’ve had the entopest wasp trap for a couple of years and found it to be really effective. Not strong enough in my opinion. Not working as well for me I have a lot of hornets in my garden but only a few in the traps after a week up
